Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows evidence of renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia (low glucose and HbA1c), and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity. These findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, metabolic derangement, and possible hepatic involvement. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are strongly recommended.
